channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Cisco’s chief product officer explains the company’s transformation around AI
item.description: For 40 years, Cisco has been best known for building routers, switches, and other networking technology that connects computers within offices, data centers, and across the internet.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: iPhone 17 Pro Max Leak Hints at Significant Redesign, September Release
item.description: A new image leak from prolific tipster @MajinBuOfficial reveals the expected redesign of the iPhone 17 Pro Max, hinting at a significant upgrade to the larger model. The leak suggests that the phone is ready for mass production, with an expected release in September 2025.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why thinking like a tech company is essential for your business’s survival
item.description: To thrive in today’s business environment, companies must align their technological and cultural foundations with their ultimate goals.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7 could be in line for a Galaxy S25 Ultra-level camera upgrade
item.description: It's been a barren few years for camera upgrades on the Z Fold series, but that might be changing in 2025.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Try these hybrid tools for thinking on paper
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. I like thinking on paper. That’s why I’ve got a box under my desk with 27 old idea notebooks.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Lip-Bu Tan Named Intel's New CEO, Aims to Restore World-Class Status
item.description: Intel confirms Lip-Bu Tan as its new CEO, replacing Pat Gelsinger. Tan aims to restore the company's world-class status by focusing on product development and foundry services.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Data Centers Pushed to Limits by AI and Cloud Services - 74% of Decision-Makers Agree
item.description: New research reveals data centers facing increased pressure due to AI demands, with companies prioritizing sustainability but facing challenges in implementation.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Marvel reportedly casts Stranger Things star Sadie Sink in Spider-Man 4, but I don't want her to tackle the roles she's rumored to play
item.description: Sadie Sink will seemingly join Tom Holland in Spider-Man's next MCU adventure – and she'll apparently play a key supporting role.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: I’m a tech pro and these are my secrets to better flights and luxury travel
item.description: Here are some travel hacks to save money on your next vacation including setting up price alerts, remaining flexible and checking for 24-hour price drops.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Intel hires former board member as new CEO in struggling chipmaker's latest comeback attempt
item.description: Struggling chipmaker Intel has hired former board member and semiconductor industry veteran Lip-Bu Tan as the latest in a succession of CEOs to attempt to turn around the company that helped define Silicon Valley.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A volcano near Alaska's largest city could erupt in the coming weeks or months, scientists say
item.description: A volcano near Alaska’s largest city is showing new signs of unrest, with experts saying the likelihood of an eruption at Mount Spurr in the next few weeks or months has increased.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: TikTok’s comment sections are being flooded with copy-pasted Christian messages
item.description: An influx of copy-and-pasted Christian messages has recently taken over TikTok’s comment sections. Over the past several days, comments about Jesus Christ have surfaced among the top comments on a wide range of unrelated videos.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Creating a cybersecurity standard of care: The future of software liability
item.description: This approach emphasizes the importance of integrating security measures into the software development lifecycle from the beginning. The post Creating a cybersecurity standard of care: The future of software liability first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Arista delivers intelligent load balancing, AI job-centric observability
item.description: Arista Networks has added load balancing and AI job-centric observability features to core software products in an effort to help enterprise customers grow and effectively manage AI networked environments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: FTC asks judge to delay Amazon trial due to resource constraints
item.description: A lawyer for the Federal Trade Commission says the agency needs to seek a trial delay in a case against Amazon due to staffing and budgetary challenges.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: OpenAI takes on rivals with new Responses API, Agents SDK
item.description: OpenAI’s new Responses API and an upgraded Agents SDK will help enterprises more easily build agents with advanced reasoning and multimodal capabilities, the company said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Archaeologists uncover the oldest known partial face fossil of a human ancestor in western Europe
item.description: Scientists report that a fossil of a partial face from a early human ancestor in Spain is between 1.1 and 1.4 million years old.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Aptori Now on Google Cloud Marketplace for AI-Powered Security and Automated Risk Remediation
item.description: Aptori’s AI-Driven AppSec Platform Proactively Eliminates Vulnerabilities to Minimize Risk and Ensure Compliance.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New tools from OpenAI help companies create their own AI agents
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday launched Responses, a new api intended to eventually replace Assistants. The big draw? Responses provides a number of new tools that companies and organizations can use to create their own AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Agentic AI – Ongoing coverage of its impact on the enterprise
item.description: Over the next few years, agentic AI is expected to bring not only rapid technological breakthroughs, but a societal transformation, redefining how we live, work and interact with the world. And this shift is happening quickly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Malware steals bank cards and passwords from millions of devices
item.description: One report claims at least 25 million people were targeted by infostealer malware between the beginning of 2023 and the end of 2024, capturing bank card details and passwords.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Google Pixel 10 could get a big camera boost if this new leak is legit
item.description: Renders purporting to show the Google Pixel 10 and Google Pixel 10 Pro XL have leaked, but by the looks of things the base-model handset is getting a third camera.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: ServiceNow’s Yokohama platform release focuses on agentic AI
item.description: ServiceNow today launched its newest Now Platform release, Yokohama, which doubles down on the company’s commitment to agentic AI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Multicloud: Tips for getting it right
item.description: More and more companies are adopting a multicloud strategy. The goal? To maximize the flexibility and performance of their IT landscapes. They combine cloud services from different providers in a targeted way to exploit the advantages of different platforms.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What you should know about the ‘invisibility cloak,’ according to the award-winning physicist who studies them
item.description: During a visit to San Diego, John Pendry said that scientists lack the specific materials they need to design a way to routinely make things 'disappear.
item.dc:creator: Gary Robbins, The Mercury News

item.title: Microsoft’s plan for genAI profits: Squeeze customers
item.description: Microsoft has ridden its multibillion-dollar investments in generative AI (genAI) to become the world’s second-most valuable company, with a valuation of roughly $3 trillion, depending on the day’s stock price.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Elon Musk’s White House role weighs on business empire
item.description: Elon Musk’s role in the Trump administration is complicating his business ventures, as the tech billionaire pushes forward with efforts to slash the federal government and budget in the face of mounting criticism.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why Wasm fascinates me
item.description: WebAssembly fascinates me. The notion of assembly language is as old as computing. Typically, assembly meant specific instructions for moving data around inside of a physical central processor. Everything in a computer is ones and zeros, but a layer above that is assembly code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump floats labeling violence against Tesla dealerships as domestic terrorism
item.description: President Trump hinted he would be open to labeling individuals who carry out violence at Tesla dealerships as “domestic terrorists” following a string of violent demonstrations at the electric vehicle company’s showrooms across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Amazon will soon be streaming Trump’s ‘The Apprentice’ reality show
item.description: The Apprentice, the long-running reality TV show that boosted Donald Trump’s profile, will begin streaming on Amazon’s Prime Video, the company announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Trump buys Tesla to 'make a statement' about Musk backlash
item.description: President Trump said Tuesday he was buying a Tesla vehicle as he doubled down on his support of Elon Musk, who has come under mounting scrutiny for leading the Trump administration's government cost initiatives. "I'm going to buy because number one, it's a great product. As good as it gets.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Nebraska Legislature approves measure classifying gig economy workers as independent contractors
item.description: The Nebraska Legislature has approved a measure classifying some gig economy workers as independent contractors, according to its website. L.B. 229, introduced by state Sen. Bob Hallstrom, was approved Monday, with 33 voting in favor and 15 opposed.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: What the breakup of Google could mean for Apple and other tech companies
item.description: Alphabet may have hoped that Donald Trump winning the White House would quell talk of a Google breakup, but that doesn’t seem likely.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company
